name: numen
description: Let Claude Desktop drive your GCU browser surfaces (weir, …) over a shared folder — no port, no extension.
long_description: Installs the numen (GCU Model Context Protocol) bridge in multi-surface watch mode: one bridge that serves every surface folder under ~/numen-desktop (one subfolder per app). Connect a surface by picking its folder + pasting your token in its connection settings; new surfaces light up automatically. Folder sharing is ON by default, so you can run more than one Desktop/Cowork session against the same surface at once. Uses the fs transport (no localhost port, no browser extension). Claude Desktop's bundled Node runtime runs it — nothing else to install. To drive a connected surface, Claude calls numen_listTools then numen_callTool.

folder.title: Surfaces folder
folder.description: Where this Desktop keeps its surface folders — one subfolder per app (e.g. <folder>/weir), each connected from the app's own settings. The default ~/numen-desktop is dedicated to Desktop (separate from ~/numen, where Claude Code agents keep theirs), so sessions can safely share it. Browse to change it; ~/... paths work.

token.title: Shared token
token.description: Your MACHINE token — the SAME value already in ~/.gcu/numen.json and pasted into each surface's connection settings. NOT a fresh secret: a new one won't match surfaces that are already running (their key is derived from the existing token). If you have no surfaces connected yet, any strong secret works as long as you paste the identical value everywhere. (Shown in plain text so you can verify it matches — it's a local token; keep it off-screen if others are watching.)

share.title: Let multiple sessions share a surface
share.description: ON (default): two or more Desktop/Cowork sessions can drive the same surface at the same time. OFF: one session at a time per folder; a second waits. Leave ON — it's safe because each surface lives under your own Surfaces folder above. Only turn OFF if you point that at a directory you also share with non-Desktop agents.
